reviews.title: Nice feel. but easily broken
reviews.text: I ordered this very inexpensive band knowing the quality would not be the best. I was surprised how comfortable the band was and thought it was a nice upgrade over the base Ionic Bands. However after wearing it for a few weeks, when putting it back on after charging, the connection to the latch broke and its not repairable. Its a nice feeling material but this broke way to easily and after only a few weeks of use is rather disappointing.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement
reviews.text: Exact replacement and a fraction of the price you would pay if you bought it from the store

reviews.title: Blue is more purple
reviews.text: The blue is more purple than it is blue. The color was mostly true to its display photo though, but just a word of caution in case if anyone really wanted a true blue. As for the band itself, I can’t say much about durability since I just installed it. This band comes with 2 securing bands whereas the original Garmin comes with 1. I also feel like the band doesn’t meet flush with the watch face as well as the original Garmin but it’s not bad. This band is also a little stiffer than the floppy Garmin band, and I think I actually like the feel of this stiffer band.

reviews.title: Fits 45s perfectly
reviews.text: Worked perfectly as a replacement band for the 45s. Not sure why other reviewers are having issues because it’s great!

reviews.title: I wanted the teal specifically
reviews.text: I replaced my watch band as soon as they came and it's been on since summer with no complaints.  I appreciate the fact that they send a little screwdriver with the set, it's so helpful not having to find something that small on your own.  Nice product!

title: Apple Watch SE (2nd Gen) (GPS, 40mm) - Starlight Aluminum Case with Starlight Sport Band, S/M (Renewed)
description: Amazon Renewed is your trusted destination for a huge selection of smartphones, computers, video games, power tools, and even more products that work and look like new and are backed by the Amazon Renewed Guarantee.
features: WHY APPLE WATCH SE — All the essentials to help you monitor your fitness, keep connected, track your health, and stay safe. Now up to 20 percent faster, with features like Crash Detection and enhanced workout metrics, it’s a better value than ever.
EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and colors, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

HEALTH AND SAFETY FEATURES — Get help when you need it with Crash Detection, Fall Detection, and Emergency SOS. Get deep insights into your health, including notifications if you have an irregular rhythm or an unusually high or low heart rate.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.
SWIMPROOF AND STYLISH — Water resistant to 50 meters. Three finishes. And a redesigned, color-matched back case made with a new production process that reduces its carbon emissions over 80 percent.
APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
STAY CONNECTED — Send a text, make a call, listen to music and podcasts, use Siri, or call for help with Emergency SOS. Apple Watch SE (GPS) works with your iPhone or Wi-Fi to keep you connected.

APPLECARE — Every Apple Watch comes with one year of hardware repair coverage through its limited warranty and up to 90 days of complimentary technical support. Get AppleCare+ to extend your coverage and reduce the stress and cost of unexpected repairs.
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS — This is a summary of the main product features. See below to learn more.

All the measurement data and results are provided for reference only and cannot be compared with the results of professional medical devices, used to perform professional diagnosis or monitoring of any medical conditions. Due to external factors (such as arm hair, tattoos, or darker skin), there may be cases where measurement results are inaccurate or even unavailable!

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

reviews.title: Great watch, much better than my old "smart" watch
reviews.text:
I got Moto 360 several years ago when smart watches first came out and it was nice, but annoying. It was tied into everything and kept giving me alerts and so eventually I got tired of it. My wife also had a first generation Samsung Gear watch and that was about the same as the Moto.
Well, fast forward a few years and now my wife, daughter, son and daughter-in-law all have newer smart watches (Apple Watches and Fitbit) and they all love them. It was time to get a new phone, so I got a Samsung S20 FE and then decided to get the Samsung watch. I saw on the reviews that it really works well with Samsung phones and so I decided to get the Watch 3. I was looking at the Watch 2, which had a few less features and then I found the Watch 3 in the 41mm size and then found a refurbished one so I ended up paying about the same for the Watch 3 as I would have for the Watch 2.
I'm very impressed with the watch and really love it. I didn't get the one with a cell phone, it just connects to my phone with bluetooth and/or wifi, but it's really amazing. I get my messages, and can turn off the ones that I don't want and then I also get all the health apps. So, now it tells me to get off my chair and walk around and does EKG and stress tesing and Oxygen levels..
I was concerned that the 41 mm might be too small, but it is a great size for me as you can see from the pic. I have had a Citizen Echo Drive for more than 10 years and it is a little larger, but I like the size of the Watch 3 better.
If you are thinking about getting a watch, this one is a good deal right now and I believe it does work best if you use it with a Samsung phone.

reviews.title: Love the watch, hate the battery.
reviews.text: I don't use the sleep tracking because the watch is bulky and annoying while trying to sleep. The mobile app is ok, constantly running in background.  The battery life sucks, only good for a day and sometimes a day and a half which is to bad because I do love all the things it does. I think I can answer my phone on my watch (still figuring out how) can definitely hang up on unwanted calls and can read messages.  Good pedometer and workout tool. Overall nice watch with crappy battery life.

reviews.title: I like it.
reviews.text: I had to change out the band. The buckle was a pain. I must recharge every day.

reviews.title: Upgrade
reviews.text: I initially gave my mom the first samsung watch and when it went down, she let me have it. Because I initially spoiled her with the first one I figured it was time to upgrade her. And she loves it, her favorite accessory thus far....

reviews.title: The price! Bought refurbished and love it
reviews.text: Love the price, as I only paid $136 because it was refurbished, but looks brand new and works great! Two things that I'm disappointed with 1 no YouTube app to listen to free music 2 must buy a subscription to Spotify! To listen to music on the watch. Besides those two negative things. The watch is a great buy

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: It’s ok

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

reviews.title: 5-star product!
reviews.text: You have to love it when a product, even something as simple as a thin piece of plastic, outperforms your expectations. I will use the Skinomi brand for my iPad, Kindle Fire and Samsung Galaxy S III as soon as their old (non-Skinomi) screen protectors need replacement.

reviews.title: Really impressed
reviews.text: Got this for my Sony LiveView and while I was shocked to see that the installation process was more than other screen protectors (ie cell phone screen protectors), installation was pretty simple. After it dries, you can't even tell a screen protector is on!

reviews.title: It's soft
reviews.text:
Like another reviewer had mentioned, they are soft. I used a rubber squeegee that I've used on countless other screen savers, and it actually damaged the skin. It has permanent "bubbles" (more like dents) in it that will not go away. Other than that, the fit is perfect. And the skin comes very nicely packaged with everything you could ever need, including a bottle of soapy water for the application.

The attention to detail by Skinomi is second to none. The company gets five stars, but I've got to deduct one for the soft material. Skinomi did include a squeegee, but it's too big for use with this skin. Had I cut it down to size and used it, I may not have "dents" now.

title: Withings Scanwatch - Hybrid Smartwatch & Activity Tracker with Connected GPS, Heart Rate Monitor, Sleep Monitor, Smart Notifications, Water Resistant with 30-day Battery Life, Android & iOS compatible
description: ScanWatch is the first hybrid smartwatch to scan vital parameters to detect heart health conditions and help improve overall fitness. ScanWatch boasts a medical-grade ECG, an oximeter for SpO2 measures, and an exceptional battery life of up to 30 days. Technologies: an electrocardiogram and an oximeter to provide on-demand essential vital parameters.
features: ELECTROCARDIOGRAM ON DEMAND — Detect atrial fibrillation or normal heart rhythm in just 30 seconds via ECG.
WRIST-BASED OXIMETRY — Provides on demand clinically validated oxygen saturation level (SpO2) in just 30 seconds.
HEART RATE TRACKING — Track heart rate continuously during workouts to optimize training, and monitor daily and overnight heart rate to help improve health over time.
TRACK BREATHING DISTURBANCES — Follow your nights and wake to signs of breathing disturbances in the app.
AUTOMATIC SLEEP MONITORING — Wake to a Sleep Score based on light & deep sleep cycles, interruptions, depth & regularity.
RECORD BATTERY LIFE — Spend more time moving and less time charging with a rechargeable battery that lasts up to 30 days.

SMART NOTIFICATIONS — Customize call, text, event & app notifications from your smartphone right on the watch screen. ScanWatch seamlessly syncs with the free Health Mate app via Bluetooth. Works with Alexa, Apple Health, Google Fit, Strava and 100+ top health & fitness apps.
SWAPPABLE WRISTBANDS — If you have multiple bands, it's easy to change them in seconds to wear ScanWatch your way: leather, silicone or metal.

reviews.title: Absolutely stellar watch but battery life is meh for a hybrid
reviews.text:
Withings wearer for almost two years here. Have had the Scanwatch for a couple months and had the Steel HR before that.

First off, if you're reading the reviews you should definitely buy either this watch or the Steel HR. I absolutely love it. It's gorgeous, the app is great, it fits in well to daily life. Looks fantastic whether suited up or wearing grubbies to work on the car. My doctor even hooked up tri-leads to me to compare the ECG with actual medical equipment and they thought it was pretty darn close! Same with O2 sat monitor and heart rate. Quality wise, on par with its excellent forerunner, the Steel HR. The functional twisting crown with haptic feedback is a nicer touch than the push button on the old one.

I have to ding it on one star because of the battery life and charge time compared to the prior model. My Steel HR lasted for about a month with all features enabled and charged almost completely in the time it took to take a shower. This one lasts about five days with all features enabled (I know, there are more features). But the bigger problem is charge time. I take the watch off for showers and that's a logical time to charge (obviously you want to wear this in your sleep). About 30 minutes does not come anywhere near to fully charging the battery. Just now it only went from 30-40%. So you need to find time to charge it during the day or occasionally not wear it while sleeping. I think I'm going to disable some of the continuous monitoring features which puts me back at my Steel HR just with sleep disturbance monitoring and O2 sat on demand.

Ultimately, if you want activity tracking, heart rate monitoring, enhanced sleep monitoring, ECG and O2 sat WHILE still wearing an attractive watch, this is hard to beat. If you don't care about ECG, O2 and sleep disturbance tracking the Steel HR is the watch to get as it's more or less the same just without those features and is cheaper. As to whether the ScanWatch is with the extra money over the Steel HR, I think that depends on how much you prioritize those extra features and battery life. If this would last a week plus on a charge I'd say the extra hundred bucks is a no brainer. But battery life as it is... tougher call.

One other note. While this watch will give you notifications, they're a bit cumbersome on the small screen. I have it turned off.

reviews.title: Screen fogged up after only a few days
reviews.text: Was exited by this smart watch, look and capabilities. But within a few short days the screen had fogged up on the inside without having come near water in any way. Very disappointing.

reviews.title: Great watch! Battery myth...
reviews.text:
The best looking, excellent build quality, thoughtful app. Notification is done nicely.

Battery Myth:

My first green horizon watch battery is very bad the battery dropped 14% on day one. I tried Amazfit, and it only drops 5% a day. I tried to disable a lot of things and it became about 7-8% a day.

Second Blue horizon, more than 24 hours later, it still shows 100%. I could not believe it. Almost like I complained and they did something remotely and manipulated the battery meter... 😊

Sleep Track is nice and main reason I bought it.

reviews.title: great appearance, perfect for work
reviews.text: I love the product. The ease of use, it's intuitive nature. It looks like a regular watch-quite stylish in my opinion, but it functions like a smart watch but with just the essentials that I need. If you are regularly using the smart watch functions with blue tooth to your phone--the battery life is closer to two weeks than a month.

reviews.title: Update:  Watch cannot keep time!  Needs frequent adjustment Not trustworthy!
reviews.text:
Update: Watch now 10 months old and does not keep accurate time. Initially a couple months ago, I noticed the time difference and using instructions adjusted it, thinking it must have been an odd glitch. I am used to watches that rarely need to be adjusted and thought a Hybrid Smartwatch would be smart and never be off time. Boy was I wrong.

Since writing the original glowing review, last May 2022 which was two weeks after purchase. I have had to adjust the time several times. The first time it was off it was 12 minutes difference. Right now it is off 5 minutes. A couple weeks ago it was off 4 minutes. This is not an isolated incident. Interestingly, the hands do not match the digital time, but all the same, both are off. Right now the hands read exactly 4:30 while the digital is 4:31 even though the real time according to my computer and Samsung S21 phone is 4:36. This is really a shame given the watch cost $300. IMO, the watch is unusable if you cannot trust it for its most basic function.

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: Battery keeps fading/ very limited watch faces and limited app.
reviews.text: Dealing with the company was good, they offered to send another watch as I do like the look of it. However, after having the battery fade after 1.5 days and screen going black and not having many watch faces I decided not to bother with it anymore.  Maybe the new one would be fine but I had spent too much time on a simple procedure with watch. Also, the app is limited, it did not show how much battery there was left which seems strange. I returned it.

reviews.title: Love my smart watch
reviews.text: I like the big round face. I am able o read my notifications. I have not figured out how to use the microphone for music and the timing on the stop watch.  The watch is very light and I love the being able to pick the different face cover on the front. The watch band is very comfortable.

reviews.title: Nice watch if it lasted even a day without needing charged.
reviews.text: I would have really enjoyed this watch if it wasn't for the terrible battery life. It won't even make it an entire day without needing charged up again.

reviews.title: Love it!!!!
reviews.text: I cant say enough about this watch!! I love it so much! All the features are great! My favorite is the tracking of how I sleep! The phone speaker works great to!

reviews.title: Best gift ever
reviews.text: Bought this for a 9 year old. It’s amazing. It has so many things on it and is easy to use. She shows it to everyone who will look at it and she feels like it’s just like her big brothers and sisters watches. Very happy.

reviews.title: 2G is Dead
reviews.text: This watch says that it utilizes a sim card with 2g service. This service is nearly dead everywhere. The sim card area in the watch is a little tricky to figure out. You have to open the back and then lift up the battery slightly. The sim card slot is located under the battery. You have to slide(left/right) and lift up the metal sim card cover like a door. Insert the sim card and lay the metal cover down over it and slide it into place to lock it. This watch is not waterproof. The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. You have to buy your own sim card and they recommend Speedtalk service. Check their coverage map for 2g service.

reviews.title: Good watch. Has sensory issues
reviews.text: Husband loves his Christmas gift. Only complaint we have is he has flick his wrist multiple times very hard to get it to light up. He plans to see if there’s  anything he can on his end to not have to that. It’s a great watch for the price.

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: This watch did everything I needed it to do.  It keeps very accurate time and has many excellent features.  The ones I was most interested in were the sleep tracker, heart and blood pressure monitor and step counter as these  are medically necessary.  The accuracy was top of the line.  My only issue is this watch caused the area on my skin that contacted the watch to get a bad rash.  While the rash was essentially harmless, it is caused by a chemical reaction in my body.  Not the watches fault, and I've experienced this side effect with other watches in the past.  Now I use it only occasionally and for only a couple of days at a time to still track the previously mentioned condition.  It's a great watch that I would recommend.

reviews.title: Quality
reviews.text: It works well but I was disappointed that I can't answer a call or cancel a incoming call from the watch.

reviews.title: The Perfect Watch
reviews.text: The mobile app is easy to navigate. The watch if very accurate with HR and steps counted. The battery lasts my husband approximately 3 weeks.

reviews.title: Nice watch for the money. Now we see how long it lasts.
reviews.text: Works good so far.

【 Excellent Quality 】- Our compatible with Fitbit Blaze Bands for men is made of 100% genuine leather material, comfortable and skin-friendly. Every band is carefully and beautifully cut. Even the band edge is smooth to touch. Reinforced and exquisite stitching ensures practicality.

【 Unique Design 】- UMAXGET Compatible for fitbit blaze bands for women, there black and silver metal frame for you to choose, both can perfectly match your Fitbit Blaze smartwatch, makes your sporty fitness tracker look like a very classic watch.

reviews.title: This band is really excellent quality. Fit is great
reviews.text:
This band is really excellent quality. Fit is great, leather is great. It is stiff (leather) at first, but that is like any leather product. It quickly adapts to your wrist and looks great. Their customer service is what I expect, top notch.

reviews.title: Looks good for the price and seems sturdy so far.
reviews.text: Looks good. Came flat and needed a few days to soften up. Not the nicest leather but this was a bargain FitBit band, not a Coach handbag. Very pleased.

reviews.title: Way too long
reviews.text: The length is way too long even though I ordered a small. Could not use it all had to send it back after putting it on my watch and trying it on and home. Too bad to because it looks really nice.

reviews.title: Very stiff

reviews.title: Nice smartwatch
reviews.text: I just got this smartwatch today. The watch face itself is not bulky, it's a little flatter, which I like. I bought this to wear in case I don't have my phone on me or beside me. Not sure how much farther away from my phone I can place a call when I'm home, but be sure to keep the bluetooth on when in use. This watch has many functions, which I have yet to use, but so far it's cute and I like it!
